titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University Department of Textiles and Apparel records23-19-2807
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Eileen Keating

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University Department of Textiles and Apparel records

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Department of Textiles and Apparel

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Department of Fiber Science and Apparel Design

did.physdesc: Syllabi, reports, minutes, publications, and audiocasettes.
did.abstract: Annual reports, faculty meeting minutes, department newsletters, committee minutes, and other papers of the department. Also, material on course syllabi, outlines and descriptions of exhibits; audio cassettes of talks.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: The origins of the department of Fiber Science and Apparel Design and the department of Design + Environmental Analysis in the College of Human Ecology can be traced back to 1925, as the Department of Household Art and the Department of Textiles and Clothing. They evolved separately and then joined together in 1969 as the Department of Design and Environmental Analysis (D+EA). They stayed together until 1985, when the Department of Textiles and Apparel split from D+EA.

The Department of Textiles and Apparel changed its name to the Department of Fiber Science and Apparel Design in the spring of 2007 and in November of 2021 the Department of Fiber Science and Apparel Design and the Department of Design and Environmental Analysis combined to become the Department of Human Centered Design.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Annual reports, faculty meeting minutes, department newsletters, committee minutes, and other papers of the department; includes a history of the evolution of the department. Also, material on course syllabi, outlines and descriptions of exhibits, featuring Mary McFadden, and Jon Haggins; audiocassettes of talks by Mary Purchase and Anita Racine.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Course Material Box 3, folders 7-17
Series II. Exhibits Box 3, folders 18-26, Box 4 folder 18
Series III. Meeting Minutes Box 1, folders 14-23, 25-26
Series IV. Newsletters & Publications Box 1, folders 27-30; Box 2 folders 10-20; Box 5; Box 6

p.ref: C. News from the Department of Textiles and Clothing

arrangement.p: Series V. Reports Box 1, folders 3, 8-17, 34; Box 2, folders 107
Series VI. Subject Files Box 1, folders 1-2, 4-7, 24, 31-33, Box 4 folders 1-11
Series VII. Audiocasette tapes tr. 7767a and tr. 7768a
Series VIII. Projects and Proposal Box 4 folders 12 - 17
Series IX. Graduate Programs Box 5 folders 1 - 4, 11

odd.head: General
odd.p: See also Cornell University Department of Fiber Science and Apparel Design records #23-19-3780

odd.head: General
odd.p: See also New York State College of Home Economics records #23-2-749

odd.head: General
odd.p: See also New York State College of Human Ecology records #23-2-2817

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Department of Textiles and Apparel records, #23-19-2807. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: There is no Box 9 in this collection.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Syllabi from 2005-2007 can be found in CUL's digital repository, eCommons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles, programs and publicity from the Cornell exhibit and various other exhibits designed by McFadden.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ten photographs with models wearing the polyester charmeuse fabric "marii" specifically dyed and pleated to McFadden's specifications and a trademark of her collections.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The country files contain photographs, articles, subject files that were used for teaching and as a resource when describing clothing and textiles in the collection.
